title: 2024 to 2026 workplan: National Advisory Committee on Immunization (NACI) - Canada.ca
content:
## National Advisory Committee on Immunization (NACI): Workplan
- Statements and publications
Canadian Immunization Guide
The following represents potential policy/program topics  to be addressed by the National Advisory Committee on Immunization (NACI) for  2026–2028. It is acknowledged that not all of these topics will be completed  over these two years and some will be carried forward to subsequent years.
The priority selections below have been informed by  consultations with NACI, the NACI Executive Committee, and Provincial and  Territorial (P/T) stakeholders. As new products and indications are authorized  in Canada, new information becomes available, or in the event of a public  health emergency, workplan items may be added for consideration and priorities  may change.
The following triggers may create items for the NACI  workplan:
- New vaccine or new indication
- Recommendation adopted by other reputable organizations
- P/T programmatic  planning needs
- New or emerging safety signal detected
- Vaccine supply issue
- Changes in disease epidemiology
- Canadian immunization Guide (CIG) chapter review
- Publication of key studies/trials
- Internal inconsistency
- Update to product monograph
- Public health emergency
Policy / Program issues
Work currently underway
Assess quadrivalent meningococcal    conjugated (Men-C-ACYW) vaccine recommendations for use in routine and    high-risk infant programs in Canada to support product transition from    Men-C-C vaccines
Vaccine supply issue
Consider a universal hepatitis B    birth dose or infant vaccination program
Recommendation adoption by reputable    organization
Statement on seasonal influenza for    2027–2028
P/T programmatic planning needs
Novel mRNA-based influenza vaccine    products, if authorised
Combination COVID-19 and influenza vaccine
Evaluate new combination vaccines for    COVID-19 and seasonal influenza in the context of existing programs, if    authorised
Assess optimal rabies vaccine    post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP) regimens, in context of guidance from the    World Health Organization (WHO)
Recommendation adoption by reputable    organization
Indigenous Health Working Group
Develop collaborative guidance on how    to incorporate Indigenous knowledge and perspectives into NACI advice for    Indigenous communities
Immunocompromised Populations Working    Group
Develop a framework to guide NACI    Working Groups in making consistent immunization recommendations for    immunocompromised populations
Internal inconsistency
Canadian Immunization Guide (CIG):    Part 1 (Key Immunization information)
Communicating effectively regarding    immunization
CIG: Part 3 (Vaccination of specific    populations)
Immunization of immunocompromised    persons
CIG: Part 4 (active vaccines)
Routine chapter updates: BCG vaccine
Part 4 chapters will be updated to    align with forthcoming NACI advice as well as from CATMAT publications based    on travel vaccines
Evaluate optimal immunization program    for COVID-19 vaccination and whether this can be extended beyond a single    season
P/T programmatic planning needs
Assess optimal use of products (MenB,    Men-C-ACYW) and schedule for routine IMD programs in Canada across age groups
P/T programmatic planning needs
Consider a universal hepatitis A    vaccine schedule, including 1-dose option
Recommendation adoption by reputable    organization
Provide guidance for a new vaccine to    prevent Lyme disease in children and adults, if submitted and authorised
Assess the administration of MMR or    MMRV vaccine during pregnancy
P/T programmatic planning needs
Assess the optimal timing for the    second dose of MMRV or MMR+V vaccine(s)
P/T programmatic planning needs
Assess the use of 4CMenB vaccine to    prevent gonorrhea in populations at increased risk of exposure
Publication of key studies/trials
Statement on seasonal influenza for    2028–2029
P/T  programmatic planning needs

P/T  programmatic planning needs

P/T  programmatic planning needs

The following chapters will be due for revision: 1) Immunization of Adults; 2) Immunization of persons with inadequate immunization records; 3) Immunization of infants born prematurely; 4) Immunization of patients in healthcare institutions; 5) Immunization of persons new to Canada; 6) Immunization of workers

Part 4 chapters requiring updates are those withnplanned NACI statements
